extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won stage 5 sprint in rain-soaked neutralized finale, timing his acceleration perfectly behind GAVIRIA Fernando's leadout with 250m remaining.
tactical_patterns: GC times taken at first finish line passage, final lap neutralized—team leaders abandoned positioning duties, creating chaotic uncontrolled sprint.
ACKERMANN Pascal deliberately braked at 250m to use GAVIRIA Fernando's power as unintended leadout, counterintuitive braking wins messy sprints.
No lead-out trains sustained speed through technical corners on wet finale circuit, individual sprinter positioning trumped team organization.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: abandoned after just 1km on stage 5, injury from previous day forced withdrawal despite no broken bones
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: timed sprint perfectly in treacherous conditions, demonstrated tactical patience to brake and reaccelerate behind rival's wheel
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: neutral: had power to lead sprint but became inadvertent leadout for winner, strong but mistimed final effort
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: consulted team car before neutralization decision, suggesting advance negotiation of GC time-taking protocol
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: neutral: also consulted team car pre-neutralization, coordinated with ROGLIČ Primož on safety protocol for dangerous finish
